question: How to create a data table in HTML
description: Build accessible, well-structured data tables using semantic HTML elements like <thead>, <tbody>, <th scope>, and <caption>.

steps.title: Use the full semantic table structure
steps.explanation: Always include <thead>, <tbody>, and optionally <tfoot>. This gives browsers and assistive technology the correct reading order and allows CSS to target each section independently.

steps.title: Add scope for accessibility
steps.explanation: The scope attribute on <th> tells screen readers whether a header applies to a column, a row, or a group. Always use scope="col" for column headers and scope="row" for row headers.

steps.title: Add a caption and summary
steps.explanation: <caption> acts as a title for the table and is the primary way screen readers identify it. Place it as the first child of <table>.

steps.title: Span columns and rows with colspan and rowspan
steps.explanation: colspan merges cells horizontally; rowspan merges them vertically. Both take a number indicating how many cells to span.
